apps.name: OpenMasjid Display
apps.tagline: Run prayer timetables, cameras and HDMI on every screen in your masjid — over your network
apps.author: hasan-ismail
apps.description:
**OpenMasjid Display** turns one small computer into the control room for every
screen in your masjid. Each TV gets its own network video link (RTSP) that you
point a cheap "RTSP decoder" box at once — then you decide, from your phone or a
computer, what each screen shows:

- **Prayer timetables** — beautiful, full-screen prayer clocks calculated on the
  device (no internet needed). Make as many as you like, each with its own colours
  to match the room it hangs in. Shows a large live clock, the Hijri and Gregorian
  dates, every prayer's Adhan **and** Iqamah time, Jumu'ah, and a gentle countdown
  to the next prayer with the current prayer highlighted.
- **Cameras** — bring in any security/IP camera or an imam camera and put it on a
  screen with one tap (great for overflow rooms and the women's section).
- **HDMI sources** — plug a laptop or a recording into an HDMI-to-network encoder
  and send it to the screens you choose.

Set it once and forget it: a weekly **schedule** can switch a screen to a camera
for Jumu'ah, then back to the timetable afterwards — automatically. Volunteers can
always take over instantly from the simple mobile page.

It's designed to be light enough for a Raspberry Pi, a mini-PC, or a Proxmox
container, and the whole control panel matches the calm OpenMasjidOS look in both
light and dark.

**Nothing to fill in here — just install.** When it's running, click **Open** —
it signs you in with your OpenMasjidOS login and matches your dashboard's light/dark
look and wallpaper. Then set everything up inside: your masjid details, your screens,
cameras and schedules. Each screen shows you the exact link to paste into its decoder —
no network address to look up.
apps.compose:
# OpenMasjid Display — one container that runs everything:
#   • the control panel (web UI + API),
#   • the timetable video renderer, and
#   • the RTSP server (MediaMTX) every screen's decoder connects to.
# Bundling them keeps it simple: one image to install, one to update. It stays
# least-privilege: no privileged mode, host networking, devices or sockets.
#
# Nothing to configure at install time: install it, open the control panel,
# create your password, and set everything up inside the app (saved to /data).
# Screens find this server automatically from the address you open the panel with.
services:
  app:
    image: ghcr.io/hasan-ismail/openmasjiddisplay:0.20.6
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      PORT: "8080"
      VOLUNTEER_PORT: "8081"
      VOLUNTEER_PUBLIC_PORT: "7861"
      DATA_DIR: /data
      # OpenMasjidOS Fabric: the platform writes these into the app's .env and runs
      # compose with --env-file, which only feeds ${VAR} SUBSTITUTION — so we must
      # reference them here for them to actually reach the container (appearance/SSO +
      # notifications). Empty defaults keep a standalone `docker compose up` quiet.
      OPENMASJID_BASE_URL: ${OPENMASJID_BASE_URL:-}
      OPENMASJID_APP_ID: ${OPENMASJID_APP_ID:-}
      OPENMASJID_APP_SECRET: ${OPENMASJID_APP_SECRET:-}
    ports:
      # host:container. OpenMasjidOS offers "Open" on the LOWEST published port,
      # so the control panel (7860) must stay below the RTSP port (8554). The
      # platform picks a different free host port automatically if one is taken.
      - "7860:8080"
      # The simple mobile volunteer page (off by default; turn it on in Settings).
      - "7861:8081"
      # RTSP video — each screen's decoder connects here (TCP).
      - "8554:8554"
    volumes:
      - data:/data

volumes:
  data:

apps.name: OpenMasjid Donations
apps.tagline: An online donation page — supporters give by entering their card details; collect and track giving.

apps.name: OpenMasjid Kiosk
apps.tagline: An Android-tablet donation kiosk — take in-person card donations with a Stripe M2 card reader.

apps.tagline: Run your madrasah — students, classes, attendance and progress.
